challenges.title: Error Hunter
challenges.objective: Find all unique ERROR types and count their occurrences
challenges.description: The production server is throwing errors. Your job is to identify what types of errors are occurring and how frequently.
hints: grep can filter for ERROR lines
sort -rn sorts numerically in reverse (highest first)
challenges.expected_pattern: numerical count followed by error type
challenges.solution: grep 'ERROR' server.log | awk '{print $NF}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn

challenges.title: Line Counter
challenges.objective: Count the number of employees (data rows, excluding header) in employees.csv
challenges.description: The HR team needs a quick count. Count only data rows, not the header.
hints: The first line is a header

challenges.title: IP Extractor
challenges.objective: List all unique IP addresses that accessed the server
challenges.description: Extract and deduplicate every IP address from the Apache access log.
hints: IPs are in the first field
challenges.expected_pattern: list of IPs one per line

challenges.title: Status Sniffer
challenges.objective: Show how many requests returned each HTTP status code
challenges.description: Understand the health of your web server by counting each status code.
hints: HTTP status is the 9th field in Apache Combined Log Format

challenges.title: Top Talkers
challenges.objective: Find the top 5 IP addresses by number of requests in nginx.log
challenges.description: Identify the clients making the most requests — could be a bot or a heavy user.
challenges.solution: awk '{print $1}' nginx.log | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -5

challenges.title: Empty Line Eraser
challenges.objective: Print the server.log with all blank lines removed
challenges.description: Clean up log files by removing empty lines for easier analysis.
hints: grep -v matches lines NOT containing a pattern
An empty line matches the regex ^$

challenges.title: Field Splitter
challenges.objective: Extract only the employee names (column 2) from employees.csv
challenges.description: Pull a single column from a CSV — a fundamental data wrangling task.
hints: The CSV uses commas as delimiters

challenges.title: Salary Analyst
challenges.objective: Find the average salary across all employees
challenges.description: Calculate the mean salary from the CSV. Column 4 is salary.
hints: END block runs after all input
Divide sum by count for average

challenges.title: Timing Violator
challenges.objective: List all paths with negative slack (timing violations), worst first
challenges.description: The VLSI timing report contains setup violations. Extract and sort them to prioritize fixing.
hints: grep can find lines with negative numbers
Sort numerically on the slack column

challenges.title: Regex Surgeon
challenges.objective: Extract all timestamps (HH:MM:SS format) from server.log
challenges.description: Parse timestamps using regex — a key skill for log timeline analysis.
hints: grep -oE extracts only the matched part

challenges.title: 404 Finder
challenges.objective: List the top 10 URLs returning 404 errors, with counts
challenges.description: Find which pages are missing and causing 404s — useful for fixing broken links.
challenges.solution: awk '$9=="404" {print $7}' apache.log | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -10

challenges.title: Sed Sculptor
challenges.objective: Replace all commas with pipes (|) in employees.csv
challenges.description: Transform the CSV delimiter — useful for piping to column or other tools.
hints: The delimiter to replace is a comma

challenges.title: Word Frequency
challenges.objective: Find the 10 most frequent words in server.log
challenges.description: Word frequency analysis is the foundation of text mining. Apply it to a server log.
challenges.solution: cat server.log | tr -s '[:space:]' '\n' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -10

challenges.title: Department Headcount
challenges.objective: Count employees per department and sort by headcount descending
challenges.description: Group employees by department and rank departments by size.

challenges.title: Liberty Cell Hunt
challenges.objective: Count the total number of cell definitions in liberty.lib
challenges.description: Liberty files define standard cell libraries. Count how many cells this library contains.
hints: Trim leading whitespace in your pattern

challenges.title: Apache Attack Detector
challenges.objective: Find IPs making more than 100 requests — potential bots or attackers
challenges.description: Identify high-volume requesters that may be scraping or attacking your site.
hints: awk can filter on a condition
challenges.solution: awk '{print $1}' apache.log | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | awk '$1>100 {print $2, $1}'

challenges.title: Awk Reporter
challenges.objective: Print a formatted report: Department | Headcount | Avg Salary
challenges.description: Combine grouping, counting, and averaging in a single awk program.
hints: Store sum and count per department
Print in END block with formatting
challenges.solution: awk -F',' 'NR>1 {dept[$3]++; sal[$3]+=$4} END {for (d in dept) printf "%-20s | %3d | %8.0f\n", d, dept[d], sal[d]/dept[d]}' employees.csv | sort

challenges.title: Clock Domain Mapper
challenges.objective: Extract all clock names and their frequencies from the SDC file
challenges.description: SDC files define design constraints. Parse the create_clock statements to build a clock inventory.
challenges.solution: grep 'create_clock' constraints.sdc | awk '{for(i=1;i<=NF;i++) if($i=="-name") print $(i+1), "period:", $(i-1+2)}' 2>/dev/null || grep 'create_clock' constraints.sdc | grep -oE '\-name [^ ]+' | awk '{print $2}'

challenges.title: Sed Pipeline
challenges.objective: Using only sed, extract lines where salary > 80000 (column 4)
challenges.description: sed is usually for substitution but it can do conditional operations too.
hints: This is tricky — sed can't do arithmetic natively
Consider chaining greps instead if needed
Or use a creative pattern match for 6-digit salaries starting with 8 or 9

challenges.title: Syslog Error Timeline
challenges.objective: Show error counts grouped by hour, formatted as HH:00 — N errors
challenges.description: Build an hourly error histogram from a syslog file — essential for incident timelines.
hints: Cut to get just the hour
Format output with printf or awk
challenges.solution: grep -i 'error' syslog.log | awk '{print $3}' | cut -d: -f1 | sort | uniq -c | awk '{printf "%s:00 — %d errors\n", $2, $1}'

challenges.title: Multi-File Merger
challenges.objective: Combine apache.log and nginx.log, sort all entries by timestamp, show the 20 most recent
challenges.description: Merge two log files and present a unified timeline — a real-world ops skill.
hints: sort can sort on a field

challenges.title: Awk State Machine
challenges.objective: Extract the QoR summary table from synthesis.log — area, power, and timing stats
challenges.description: Synthesis logs contain structured reports buried in output. Use awk to parse state transitions and extract the summary table.
hints: awk can track whether you're 'inside' a section
Print only lines between the markers

challenges.title: Sed Address Book
challenges.objective: Using sed, number every data line as 'N. Name, Dept, Salary' (skip header)
challenges.description: Combine line numbering and field formatting using sed's advanced features.
hints: Or use sed with a counter variable... actually awk is easier here
Challenge: do it in pure sed using branching

challenges.title: Pipeline Architect
challenges.objective: One-liner: top 5 IPs, their total bytes transferred, and their most common status code
challenges.description: Build a complex multi-dimensional analysis pipeline combining awk associative arrays.
hints: Track multiple stats per IP in awk arrays
bytes field is $10 in Apache Combined Log
Print formatted summary in END block
challenges.solution: awk '{ip[$1]++; bytes[$1]+=$10; status[$1][$9]++} END {for(i in ip) {max=0; for(s in status[i]) if(status[i][s]>max){max=status[i][s];top=s}; print ip[i], bytes[i], top, i}}' apache.log | sort -rn | head -5

challenges.title: Regex Maestro
challenges.objective: Extract all unique User-Agent strings from apache.log and classify: browser vs bot vs other
challenges.description: User-agent parsing is essential for traffic analysis. Build a classifier using grep and awk.
hints: User-Agent is in quotes near the end of each log line
grep -oP with a Perl regex can extract quoted strings
Classify based on keywords: Mozilla, bot, crawler
challenges.expected_pattern: classified user agent types with counts
challenges.solution: awk -F'"' '{print $6}' apache.log | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -20

challenges.title: The Grand Pipeline
challenges.objective: Compute: requests/min rate for each unique URL, show top 10 busiest endpoints
challenges.description: This requires parsing timestamps, computing rates, and ranking — all in one pipeline. This is what senior engineers do in production.
challenges.expected_pattern: top 10 URLs with requests per minute
challenges.solution: awk '{url=$7; gsub(/\?.*$/,"",url); count[url]++} END {for(u in count) print count[u], u}' apache.log | sort -rn | head -10

challenges.title: VLSI Timing Closure
challenges.objective: For each violated path: show endpoint, slack, and suggest minimum fix (negate slack = required improvement in ps)
challenges.description: Real timing closure work. Parse violated paths, compute required fixes, format a fix-priority report.
hints: Each violation has endpoint and slack
challenges.solution: grep 'VIOLATED' timing.rpt | awk '{for(i=1;i<=NF;i++) if($i~/^-[0-9]/) slack=$i; print slack, "fix needed:", -slack+0, "ps"}' | sort -n | head -20

challenges.title: Log Correlation
challenges.objective: Find all IPs that hit a 500 error AND also made more than 50 total requests — cross-correlate two datasets
challenges.description: Real incident response: find misbehaving clients who are both heavy users AND causing errors.
hints: You need two passes or two awk arrays
Second track: IPs with >50 total requests
challenges.solution: awk '{count[$1]++; if($9=="500") bad[$1]=1} END {for(ip in bad) if(count[ip]>50) print ip, count[ip]}' apache.log | sort -k2 -rn
